215	\end{remark}
216	\begin{lemma}
217	\leanok
218	[Dense-open agreement: the geometric heart of the Rigidity Lemma]
219	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
221	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
222	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
223	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
224	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
225	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
226	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
227	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
228	  dense-open construction is the body of that proof).}
229	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety and \(Y\), \(Z\) any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
230	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a point \(x_0 \in X\), and suppose given
231	  \(y_0 \in Y\), \(z_0 \in Z\) with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}
232	  \[ f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \qquad
233	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
234	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
235	  Then there is a non-empty open subset \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) on which \(f\) agrees, as a
236	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\), where
237	  $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
238	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)\( is the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$'' endomorphism. Concretely
239	  \(U = X \times V\) with \(V := Y - G\), \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))\) for an affine open
240	  neighbourhood \(U_0\) of \(z_0\), and on \(U\) one has \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)\).
241	  \medskip
242	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of \(V\) is exactly
243	  the assertion \(y_0 \notin G\), and this holds \emph{because} $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
244	  \subseteq U_0\( keeps the slice over \)y_0\( inside the affine \)U_0\(, away from \)F = Z - U_0$. A
245	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for \(X = Y = Z\) and
246	  \(f = p_1\) the first projection, \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0\)
247	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in \(\{x = x_0\}\), which has empty
248	  interior in the irreducible \(X \times Y\)). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
249	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
250	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.
251	  \medskip
252	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
253	  source.} The prose already takes \(\bar k\) algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
254	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
255	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field \(\kappa(y)\) to be \(\bar k\) itself, so that a
256	  proper integral slice has global sections \(\Gamma = \bar k\) and maps to a single \(\bar k\)-point of
257	  the affine \(U_0\); this requires \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\). Second, the globalisation of the
258	  per-slice agreement (Step~2 of the bridge, \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}) needs the
259	  closed points of the saturated open \(U\) to be \emph{dense} --- i.e.\ \(U\) to be a Jacobson space.
260	  Over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) this holds precisely because \(X \times Y\) is \emph{locally of
261	  finite type} over \(\bar k\) (\texttt{LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace} transports the Jacobson
262	  property of \(\Spec \bar k\), and \texttt{closure\_closedPoints} then gives density); this requires
263	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\). Accordingly the formalization carries
264	  \emph{both} \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) and
265	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) on this lemma (in addition to
266	  \([\mathtt{Field}\ \bar k]\)), and propagates the same two instances to \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma},
267	  its core helper \texttt{rigidity\_core}, and the bridge sub-lemmas
268	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} and
269	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}. Both cost nothing downstream: the
270	  consumers \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} and \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (Lean
271	  \texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}) \emph{already} assume
272	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) --- the base-field variable is literally named \(\mathtt{kbar}\) ---
273	  and curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over \(\bar k\), so the
274	  locally-of-finite-type instance is automatic there.
275	\end{lemma}
276	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
277	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
278	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
279	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
280	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
281	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
282	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
283	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
284	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
285	% assertion."
286	\begin{proof}
287	  \leanok
288	  Let \(U_0\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U_0\), and set
289	  \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a
290	  \emph{closed} map, so \(G\) (the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\)) is closed in \(Y\). The
291	  collapse hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U_0\) gives
292	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), hence \(y_0 \notin G\); this is the only place
293	  the collapse hypothesis is used, and it is essential, for \(V := Y - G\) is non-empty precisely
294	  because \(y_0 \in V\). For each \(y \in V\) the proper variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is mapped by \(f\)
295	  into the affine \(U_0\), hence to a single point, necessarily \(f(x_0, y)\). So for all \(x \in X\),
296	  \(y \in V\), we have \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x, y)\), i.e.\ \(f\) and
297	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on the non-empty open \(U := X \times V\). (In the Lean
298	  encoding the two slice-constancy bridges are the closed-map step from properness and the
299	  ``proper-into-affine has single-point image'' step; see the docstring of
300	  \texttt{rigidity\_core}.)
301	  \medskip
302	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization notes (the concrete char-free Mathlib route).} The textbook
303	  proof above is realised in Lean by three pieces. They cite Mathlib lemma names as the chosen
304	  route; none uses cohomology, and the relative Stein-factorisation framing is deliberately
305	  \emph{avoided} (see below).
306	  \emph{Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) --- BUILT.} ``\(X\) complete \(\Rightarrow p_2\) closed'' is
307	  the axiom-clean helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap} (iter-158). It is obtained from
308	  \texttt{IsProper.toUniversallyClosed} on \(X\), base-changed across the canonical pullback square
309	  to make \(p_2\) universally closed, using that the monoidal projection \emph{is} the chosen
310	  pullback projection: \texttt{Over.snd\_left} rewrites \((\mathtt{snd}\,X\,Y).\mathtt{left}\) to
311	  \(\mathtt{Limits.pullback.snd}\ X.\mathtt{hom}\ Y.\mathtt{hom}\) exactly. This step is already
312	  proven.
313	  \emph{The fibre fact (non-emptiness of \(V\)) --- RESOLVED route, char-free.} That the slice
314	  \(X \times \{y_0\}\) over the \(\bar k\)-rational point \(y_0\) is exactly the image of the slice
315	  section \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) is formalised \emph{without} the residue-field /
316	  \texttt{Triplet} / tensor-product machinery (that fine \texttt{carrierEquiv} layer is the wrong
317	  granularity and is to be avoided). One pastes the identity square for the section against the
318	  canonical pullback square (\texttt{IsPullback.of\_right},
319	  \texttt{IsPullback.of\_horiz\_isIso}; the section identities \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1\) and
320	  \(s \fatsemi p_2 = X.\mathtt{hom} \fatsemi y_0\) come from \texttt{lift\_fst}/\texttt{lift\_snd}
321	  with \texttt{Over.w} on the unit) and reads off the fibre with the \emph{coarse} topological
322	  layer of \texttt{PullbackCarrier}, namely
323	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pullback.image\_preimage\_eq\_of\_isPullback}. This needs no
324	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}]\).
325	  \emph{Bridge~2 (slice-constancy / the agreement equation) --- RESOLVED route, cohomology-FREE.}
326	  ``Proper connected slice into affine \(\Rightarrow\) single point, glued to a morphism equality on
327	  \(U\)'' is realised by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route, \emph{not} by any
328	  relative Stein-factorisation or proper-pushforward \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) statement: the
329	  latter is a genuine Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no \(H^0\) base change), and route~(c)
330	  exists precisely to avoid coherent cohomology, so it must \emph{not} be attempted. The concrete
331	  stack is: for each closed point \(y \in V\) one has \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\) (alg-closed, finite type);
332	  the slice \(X_y\) is proper integral, so its global sections form a field
333	  (\texttt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}) module-finite over \(\bar k\)
334	  (\texttt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}), and algebraic closedness kills the finite
335	  extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring
336	  map on global sections (\texttt{ext\_of\_isAffine}), so \(f\) and
337	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on each closed slice. Closed points are dense in the
338	  locally-of-finite-type \(U\) (\texttt{closure\_closedPoints} / the Jacobson-space property of
339	  locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-schemes; in the formalization this density is supplied by the new
340	  hypothesis \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)), so the per-slice
341	  agreement globalises to all of \(U\) via the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity
342	  \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} (the same handle \texttt{rigidity\_core} already
343	  uses). The packaged ``morphisms agreeing on a dense set of closed points are equal''
344	  hom-extensionality --- the one connective Mathlib does not supply directly (it gives only the
345	  single-dominant-morphism \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}) --- has now been built as the axiom-clean
346	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2): it assembles the closed points into one
347	  dominant coproduct probe and applies \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}. The whole argument is extracted
348	  into the dedicated obligation \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} below, whose body
349	  wires that proven Step~2 over the per-slice Step~1
350	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}); Step~1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice
351	  constancy) is now the chain's \emph{single} genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
352	\end{proof}
353	\begin{lemma}
354	\leanok
355	[Bridge~2: slice-constancy on a saturated open mapping into an affine]
356	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
357	  
358	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
359	  % NOTE (iter-161): the iter-160 SIGNATURE GAP is RESOLVED. The Lean proof wires Step 2
360	  % (morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints, PROVEN axiom-clean) over Step 1
361	  % (rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine, the residual sorry). Step 2 needs the closed
362	  % points of U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson; over k̄ this follows from (X⊗Y) being locally of finite
363	  % type, so [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom] is now a stated hypothesis of this lemma (and across the
364	  % chain). With it, the `JacobsonSpace U` instance is a routine discharge (LocallyOfFiniteType.
365	  % jacobsonSpace + JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding), no longer an as-typed gap. The only `sorry`
366	  % reachable from this lemma is now Step 1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy). The hypothesis
367	  % set is recorded in the statement and proof prose below (both [IsAlgClosed k̄] AND
368	  % [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom]); the earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim
369	  % is retired.
370	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
371	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
372	  % \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
373	  % any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
374	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
375	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
376	  ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
377	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(X\), \(Y\), \(Z\) be objects of
378	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) with \(X\) proper, \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible, reduced,
379	  and \emph{locally of finite type} over \(\bar k\) (the formalization carries
380	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\), which makes the saturated open \(U\)
381	  below a Jacobson space, so its closed points are dense --- the hypothesis Step~2 of the proof
382	  consumes), and \(Z\) separated. Let \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a \(\bar k\)-point
383	  \(x_0 \colon \mathbf 1 \to X\), and write $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
384	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)\( for the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$''
385	  endomorphism. Suppose:
386	  \begin{itemize}
387	    \item \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) is a \(p_2\)-\emph{saturated} open, i.e.\ \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\)
388	      for a set \(V_{\mathrm{set}} \subseteq Y\) (so \(U\) contains the whole fibre \(X_y\) over each of
389	      its points);
390	    \item \(U_0 \subseteq Z\) is an \emph{affine} open;
391	    \item \(f(U) \subseteq U_0\), i.e.\ \(f\) maps \(U\) into the affine \(U_0\).
392	  \end{itemize}
393	  Then \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on \(U\) as scheme morphisms:
394	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\).
395	\end{lemma}
396	\begin{proof}
397	  \leanok
398	  This is the formalization realization of Mumford's step ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete
399	  variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single
400	  point of \(U\)''. We realise it by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route that is
401	  \emph{cohomology-free}; in particular the relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
402	  \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no
403	  \(H^0\) base change) and is \emph{deliberately avoided}. The argument splits into two named
404	  sub-lemmas, one residual and one proven.
405	  \medskip
406	  \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (per-closed-slice constancy) --- \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}, the residual.}
407	  Fix a closed point \(x\) of \(U\), lying over a closed point \(y = p_2(x) \in V_{\mathrm{set}}\).
408	  Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and \(Y\) is of finite type over \(\bar k\), the residue field
409	  is \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\). Because \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\) is saturated, \(U\) contains the
410	  \emph{entire} fibre \(X_y \cong X\) over \(y\); hence \(f\) maps the proper integral slice \(X_y\) into the
411	  affine \(U_0\). By \(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\) the global sections \(\Gamma(X_y)\) form a
412	  field, module-finite over \(\bar k\) by \(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\), and
413	  algebraic closedness collapses the finite extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into
414	  the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring map on global sections (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), so the
415	  restriction of \(f\) to \(X_y\) factors through a single \(\bar k\)-point of \(U_0\) --- necessarily the
416	  point \(f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x)\). Thus \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\)
417	  agree (after the residue-field probe) at every closed point of \(U\). This per-slice statement is the
418	  chain's single genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
419	  \medskip
420	  \noindent\textbf{Step 2 (globalisation over dense closed points) --- \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, proven.}
421	  The closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-scheme \(U\)
422	  (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\) / the Jacobson-space property, supplied here by the new hypothesis
423	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)). To turn ``\(f\) and
424	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree at each closed point'' into a single morphism equality on \(U\),
425	  the proven globalisation \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} assembles the closed points
426	  into one dominant probe --- the coproduct
427	  \(\coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,U} \Spec \kappa(x) \to U\), whose range is dense --- and feeds
428	  it to the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity handle \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (the same
429	  family \(\mathtt{rigidity\_core}\) uses). This yields the morphism equality
430	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\) on all of \(U\). The
431	  ``agrees on a dense set of closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-equality'' connective is the one piece
432	  Mathlib does not package directly (it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
433	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\)); that bespoke build is now done in
434	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, and it is \emph{not} cohomology. \qedhere
435	\end{proof}
436	\begin{lemma}\leanok
437	[Step~2: morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a Jacobson source are equal]
438	  \label{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
439	  
440	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
441	  % Project-bespoke assembly: NO external source. Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
442	  % ext_of_isDominant; the "dense closed points => hom-extensionality" connective is built here.
443	  Let \(W\), \(Z\) be schemes with \(W\) \emph{reduced}, \(W\) a \emph{Jacobson space}, and \(Z\)
444	  \emph{separated}. Let \(g_1, g_2 \colon W \to Z\) be morphisms. If, for every \emph{closed} point
445	  \(x \in W\), the two morphisms agree after restriction along the residue-field point
446	  \(\Spec \kappa(x) \to W\) (in Lean, $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 =
447	  W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\(), then \)g_1 = g_2$.
448	\end{lemma}
449	\begin{proof}
450	\leanok
451	  This is the ``dense closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-extensionality'' connective that Mathlib does
452	  not package directly: it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism extensionality
453	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (a dominant morphism into a separated target is an epimorphism for
454	  the purpose of right-cancellation). We assemble the per-closed-point hypotheses into one such
455	  dominant probe.
456	  Form the coproduct \(P := \coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} \Spec \kappa(x)\) and the morphism
457	  \(\mathrm{probe} \colon P \to W\) defined by \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\) of the residue-field points
458	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\). Its topological range contains every closed point of \(W\):
459	  the inclusion \(\iota_x \colon \Spec \kappa(x) \to P\) composes with \(\mathrm{probe}\) to
460	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) (the defining property of \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\)), and the
461	  range of \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) is the singleton \(\{x\}\)
462	  (\(\mathtt{Scheme.range\_fromSpecResidueField}\)). Because \(W\) is a Jacobson space, the closed points
463	  are dense (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\), i.e.\ \(\overline{\mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} = W\)), so
464	  the range of \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dense and \(\mathrm{probe}\) is \emph{dominant}.
465	  Componentwise, \(g_1\) and \(g_2\) agree after \(\mathrm{probe}\): precomposing with each coproduct
466	  inclusion \(\iota_x\) reduces (by \(\mathtt{Sigma.\iota\_desc}\)) to
467	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 = W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\),
468	  which is the hypothesis at the closed point \(x\). By the universal property of the coproduct
469	  (\(\mathtt{Sigma.hom\_ext}\)) this gives \(\mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_1 = \mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_2\).
470	  Since \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dominant and \(Z\) is separated, \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) cancels it
471	  on the left to yield \(g_1 = g_2\).
472	\end{proof}
473	\begin{lemma}
474	\leanok
475	[Global-sections constancy: two \(\bar k\)-points of a proper integral source into an affine agree]
476	  \label{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
478	  % Project-bespoke assembly of Mathlib lemmas: NO external source. Realises, cohomology-free, the
479	  % classical "a global regular function on a proper integral variety over an algebraically closed
480	  % field is constant" --- precisely the affine-image step of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma proof quoted
481	  % above (the "complete slice into an affine maps to a single point" sentence, Ch.~II \S4, p.~43).
482	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(W\) be an integral scheme equipped with a structure
483	  morphism \(w \colon W \to \Spec \bar k\) that is universally closed (proper) and locally of finite
484	  type. Let \(g \colon W \to V\) be a morphism into an \emph{affine} scheme \(V\). Then any two
485	  \(\bar k\)-points of \(W\) --- i.e.\ any two sections \(a, b \colon \Spec \bar k \to W\) of \(w\) (so
486	  \(a \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\) and \(b \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\)) --- satisfy \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\).
487	  \medskip
488	  \noindent\textbf{Every hypothesis is load-bearing} (confirmed by the iter-161 audit).
489	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) collapses the finite extension \(\Gamma(W)/\bar k\); integrality of
490	  \(W\) makes \(\Gamma(W)\) a domain (without it a two-point disjoint union is a counterexample);
491	  \(\mathtt{UniversallyClosed}\) is what upgrades \(\Gamma(W)\) to a \emph{field} (without it
492	  \(\mathbb A^1\), with \(\Gamma = \bar k[t]\) and two distinct \(\bar k\)-points, is a counterexample);
493	  \(\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\) gives finiteness of \(\Gamma(W)\) over \(\bar k\); and
494	  \([\mathtt{IsAffine}\ V]\) is what lets \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\) pin the morphism by its ring map
495	  on global sections.
496	\end{lemma}
497	\begin{proof}
498	\leanok
499	  Because \(W\) is integral and \(w\) is universally closed, the global sections
500	  \(\Gamma(W, \mathcal O_W)\) form a field (\(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\)), and the structure
501	  ring map \(w^\sharp \colon \bar k \to \Gamma(W)\) (the map \(\mathtt{wk.appTop}\) after the canonical
502	  identification \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k) \cong \bar k\)) is integral / module-finite
503	  (\(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\)). Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and
504	  \(\Gamma(W)\) is a domain finite over it, that map is bijective
505	  (\(\mathtt{IsAlgClosed.ringHom\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}\)); thus \(\Gamma(W) = \bar k\) and
506	  \(w^\sharp\) is an isomorphism on global sections --- in particular an epimorphism. Both sections
507	  \(a\), \(b\) left-invert \(w^\sharp\) on global sections (they are sections of \(w\)), so they induce the
508	  \emph{same} ring map \(a^\sharp = b^\sharp\) on \(\Gamma(W)\) (cancel the epimorphism \(w^\sharp\)). A
509	  morphism into the \emph{affine} \(V\) is pinned by its global-sections ring map
510	  (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), whence \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\). This realises, without
511	  cohomology, the classical ``proper connected variety into an affine is a single point'' step
512	  quoted from Mumford above.
513	\end{proof}
514	\begin{lemma}
515	\leanok
516	[Integrality descends to a retract of an integral scheme]
517	  \label{lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract}
519	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) project-bespoke (elementary topology + algebra); NO external source.
520	  % LANDED axiom-clean as `AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract`, stated generically for any
521	  % retract `r : S ⟶ T`, `pr : T ⟶ S` with `r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S` of an integral `T` (more general than
522	  % the `X`/`X×Y`/`p_1` instance below). PROOF DIVERGENCE (cosmetic, sound either way): the Lean
523	  % proves reducedness PER-STALK (each `S.stalk x` embeds via `pr.stalkMap` into the reduced
524	  % `T.stalk`, then `isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`), not via the global-sections split-injection
525	  % the prose below describes. Plan/blueprint-writer may align the prose to the stalk route.
526	  Let \(X \times Y\) be an integral scheme (here it is geometrically irreducible and reduced over
527	  \(\bar k\)). Suppose \(X\) is a \emph{retract} of \(X \times Y\): there is a section
528	  \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) of the first projection \(p_1\), i.e.\ \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X\).
529	  Then \(X\) is integral.
530	\end{lemma}
531	\begin{proof}
532	\leanok
533	  Integrality is reducedness together with irreducibility, and the retract supplies each.
534	  \emph{Reduced.} The section identity \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X\) makes the pullback on global
535	  sections \(p_1^\sharp \colon \mathcal O_X(X) \to \mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)\) split
536	  injective (it is left-inverted by \(s^\sharp\)). A split-injective ring map into the reduced ring
537	  \(\mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)\) embeds \(\mathcal O_X(X)\) into a reduced ring, so it too is
538	  reduced (\(\mathtt{isReduced\_of\_injective}\) on the split injection); hence \(X\) is reduced.
539	  \emph{Irreducible.} The first projection \(p_1 \colon X \times Y \to X\) is surjective because it
540	  admits the section \(s\) (every point of \(X\) is \(p_1(s(\cdot))\)). The continuous image of an
541	  irreducible space under a surjection is irreducible; as \(X \times Y\) is irreducible and \(p_1\) is a
542	  continuous surjection onto \(X\), the space of \(X\) is irreducible.
543	  Reduced and irreducible together give that \(X\) is integral. This is elementary --- no cohomology,
544	  no external citation --- and feeds the Step-1 geometric assembly below, where the proper slice
545	  \(X_y \cong X\) must be presented as proper \emph{integral}.
546	\end{proof}

774	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
775	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
776	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group
777	scheme). Then every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` killing a `k̄`-point `p` (`p ≫ f = η[A]`) equals the
778	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
779	
780	This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that `genusZeroWitness` consumes
781	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
782	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
783	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with
784	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`ℙ¹ → A` constant) and pin the constant value to `η[A]` via
785	the pointed hypothesis. No `df = 0`, no Serre duality, no Picard representability.
786	
787	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
788	(RR bridge, iter-167+) and `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s helper residuals. Once the RR
789	bridge closes and the helper's product-instance + dominance sorries discharge, lifts to
790	axiom-clean. -/
791	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
792	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
793	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
794	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
795	    [IsProper C.hom]
796	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
797	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
798	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
799	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
800	    (f : C ⟶ A)
801	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
802	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
803	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
804	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
805	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
806	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
807	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
808	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
809	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
810	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
811	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
812	    exact _hf
813	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
814	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
815	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
816	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
817	    exact hpoint
818	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
819	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
820	  calc f
821	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
822	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
823	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
824	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
825	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
826	        rw [← Category.assoc,
827	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]

/-- **Sub-task f section (iter-183 Lane E):** the chart-1 section
`s : Gm.left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover).X 1`, built as the `pullback.lift` over
`pullback q (awayι (![X 0, X 1] 1))` with the iotaGm-prefix as `q`-component and
`Gm.hom ≫ r_1` as `awayι`-component. -/
private noncomputable def iotaGm_chart1_section (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (r_1 : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))))
    (h_r_1 : r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1) =
      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left) :
    (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X (1 : Fin 2) :=
  pullback.lift
    (pullback.lift
      ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left)
      (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left)
      (iotaGm_inner_lift_compat kbar))
    ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ r_1)
    (by
      simp [pullback.lift_fst, ← h_r_1, Category.assoc]
      rfl)

`iotaGm_r_1` via `unfold` without re-deriving the lift identity through `cancel_mono`. -/
private noncomputable def iotaGm_chart1_section (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X (1 : Fin 2) :=
  pullback.lift
    (pullback.lift

      rfl)

content: The file /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

250	and applies `ext_of_isAffine` to reduce to a global-sections ring map equation.
251	The residual is a fully concrete `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext`-style chase through
252	public Mathlib `appTop` simp lemmas (`Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`,
253	`pullbackSpecIso_hom_*`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_*`,
254	`pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_*`, `pullback.lift_*`,
255	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap`). iter-186+ closure target. -/
256	private lemma iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
257	    IsOpenImmersion (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar
258	      ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2)) := by
259	  -- The composition equals the canonical inclusion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] → ℙ¹` sending
260	  -- `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`, which factors as three open immersions:
261	  --   Spec(GmRing) → Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) → ℙ¹.
262	  -- The first is `Spec.map (algebraMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))`, an open
263	  -- immersion via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization` (Mathlib).
264	  -- The second is `Spec.map homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.toRingHom`, an iso (hence
265	  -- open immersion).
266	  -- The third is `Proj.awayι (X 1)`, an open immersion (Mathlib instance).
267	  -- Step 1: the canonical 3-step open immersion is set up explicitly.
268	  -- `Gm.left → Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar)` via the localization algebraMap.
269	  haveI h_loc : IsOpenImmersion
270	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
271	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)))) :=
272	    IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization
273	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
274	  -- `Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar) → Spec(Away 𝒜 X_1)` via the chart-ring iso.
275	  -- `RingEquiv` lifts to a `CommRingCat` iso whose `.hom = CommRingCat.ofHom (toRingHom)`,
276	  -- so the latter is `IsIso`, and `Spec.map` preserves isos.
277	  haveI h_iso_isIso : IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom
278	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) := by
279	    rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom
280	          = (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.hom from rfl]
281	    exact (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.isIso_hom
282	  haveI h_iso : IsOpenImmersion (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
283	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom)) :=
284	    inferInstance
285	  -- Reduce to the substantive equation: `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
286	  -- Once established, the conclusion follows from `IsOpenImmersion.comp`
287	  -- applied three times (one per factor of the canonical chain).
288	  haveI h_proj : IsOpenImmersion (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
289	      (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
290	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos) :=
291	    inferInstance
292	  suffices h_eq : iotaGm_chart1_section kbar r_1 h_r_1
293	        ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) =
294	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
295	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
296	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
297	          (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) ≫
298	          Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
299	            (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
300	            (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos by
301	    rw [h_eq]
302	    -- Composition of three open immersions is an open immersion.
303	    exact @IsOpenImmersion.comp _ _ _ _ _ h_loc
304	      (@IsOpenImmersion.comp _ _ _ _ _ h_iso h_proj)
305	  -- Prove the equation `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
306	  -- Stage 1: Fuse the two trailing `Spec.map`s on the RHS into a single `Spec.map`
307	  --   via `Spec.map_comp` + `CommRingCat.ofHom_comp`, so both sides end with
308	  --   `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f_ring) ≫ Proj.awayι (X 1)` where
309	  --   `f_ring = (algMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
310	  --             (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom`.
311	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
312	  -- Stage 2: Unfold `gmScalingP1_chart` on the LHS and re-associate so both
313	  --   sides end with `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Apply `congr 1` to cancel the
314	  --   trailing `Proj.awayι (X 1)` factor (open immersion ⟹ mono, but `congr 1`
315	  --   is structural and avoids needing the mono instance directly).
316	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
317	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
318	  congr 1
319	  -- Residual goal: identify the LHS prefix (section ≫ cover_X_iso 1).hom ≫
320	  -- Spec.map (eval₂Hom_chart1_ringMap ∘ iso.toRingHom)) with the RHS prefix
321	  -- `Spec.map(f_ring) : Spec(GmRing) → Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))`.
322	  -- Both sides go between affine schemes. The RHS is a single `Spec.map`;
323	  -- the LHS reduces to a `Spec.map` once we unfold `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
324	  -- (private in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`) and use `pullbackSpecIso`
325	  -- + `pullback.lift_fst`/`_snd` simp lemmas to identify the underlying ring
326	  -- map `Away 𝒜 X_1 ⊗ GmRing → GmRing` sending
327	  --   a ⊗ 1 ↦ algebraMap kbar GmRing (r_1_ring(a))
328	  --   1 ⊗ b ↦ b
329	  -- (with `r_1_ring : Away X_1 → kbar` the ring map underlying `r_1`, sending
330	  -- the chart-1 affine coord `isLocalizationElem = X_0/X_1` to `1 ∈ kbar`).
331	  --
332	  -- Composed with the chart-1 ring map (= `eval₂Hom(X() ↦ isLocElem ⊗ algMap X())
333	  -- ∘ iso.toRingHom`), the underlying ring map collapses to
334	  -- `algMap MvPoly GmRing ∘ iso.toRingHom = f_ring` by:
335	  --   isLocalizationElem ↦ (eval₂Hom ∘ iso)(isLocalizationElem)
336	  --                       = isLocElem ⊗ algMap X()
337	  --                       ↦ μ(isLocElem ⊗ algMap X())
338	  --                       = μ(isLocElem ⊗ 1) · μ(1 ⊗ algMap X())
339	  --                       = algMap kbar GmRing(r_1_ring(isLocElem)) · algMap MvPoly GmRing(X())
340	  --                       = algMap kbar GmRing(1) · t = t = algMap MvPoly GmRing(X())
341	  --                       = f_ring(isLocalizationElem).
342	  -- Constants follow analogously via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap`.
343	  --
344	  -- Stage 3 (iter-185 attack): the `(gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar 1).hom` in the LHS is
345	  -- inaccessible to `unfold` (the def is `private` in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`),
346	  -- but its body is a 4-step iso chain `pullbackSymmetry ≪≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ≪≫
347	  -- pullback.congrHom _ _ ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso`, with the two `pullback.congrHom`-equality
348	  -- proofs `Prop`-typed (hence proof-irrelevant). A `change` with `_, _` placeholders for
349	  -- those two proofs forces Lean to reconstruct them by unification and lets the kernel
350	  -- discharge the defEq via proof irrelevance — sidestepping the private-name barrier.
351	  change (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar r_1 h_r_1 ≫
352	      (pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
353	        pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
354	        pullback.congrHom _ _ ≪≫
355	        pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)).hom) ≫ _ = _
356	  -- Stage 4: spread the iso (4 components) and unfold the section's `pullback.lift`
357	  -- skeleton so the LHS becomes a fully concrete chain
358	  --   `pullback.lift _ _ _ ≫ pullbackSymmetry.hom ≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso.hom ≫
359	  --    pullback.congrHom.hom ≫ pullbackSpecIso.hom ≫ Spec.map (eval₂Hom_comp_iso)`.
360	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc, iotaGm_chart1_section]
361	  -- Stage 5: pull the trailing iso `Spec.map (iso.toRingHom)` out via `Spec.map_comp` on
362	  -- both sides so both end with the SAME morphism `Spec.map(iso.toRingHom)`. With the
363	  -- iso instance `h_iso_isIso` (= `IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom iso.toRingHom)`),
364	  -- `Spec.map(iso.toRingHom)` is itself iso; `cancel_iso_hom_right_assoc` cancels it.
365	  rw [CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, Spec.map_comp, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, Spec.map_comp]
366	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
367	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom)) := inferInstance
368	  -- Stage 6: fully left-associate `≫`s so both sides syntactically end with the iso
369	  -- `Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom`; then `cancel_mono`
370	  -- on the iso (`IsIso → Mono`) discards the trailing factor.
371	  simp only [← Category.assoc]
372	  apply (CategoryTheory.cancel_mono (f := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
373	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom))).mpr
374	  -- Stage 7: both sides now target the affine `Spec (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Use
375	  -- `ext_of_isAffine` to reduce to ring-map equality on global sections.
376	  refine ext_of_isAffine ?_
377	  -- Stage 8 (iter-188 6-step appTop recipe execution per progress-critic):
378	  --
379	  -- After `ext_of_isAffine`, the goal is the `appTop` equation
380	  --   `appTop(LHS chain) = appTop(Spec.map(algMap MvPoly→GmRing))`
381	  -- both viewed as `CommRingCat` morphisms `Γ(Spec(MvPoly Unit kbar)) ⟶ Γ(Spec(GmRing))`.
382	  --
383	  -- The 6 steps:
384	  --   (1) Add helper `r_1_appTop_isLocElem_eq_one : r_1.appTop(isLocElem) = 1` via
385	  --       `cancel_mono` on `Proj.awayι` + `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` chain — see
386	  --       inline `have h_r_1_appTop_isLocElem` below.
387	  --   (2) Telescope `comp_appTop` (`Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`).
388	  --   (3) Telescope `ΓSpecIso_naturality` for the `Spec.map` factors.
389	  --   (4) Apply `pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst/snd` for the spec ⊗ pullback iso.
390	  --   (5) Apply `pullback.lift_fst/snd` for the section's nested pullback.lift.
391	  --   (6) Discharge residual via the Step 1 helper.
392	  --
393	  -- iter-188 testing status — the 6-step simp chain telescopes (default `simp`
394	  -- after `ext_of_isAffine` reduces `comp_appTop`, `Iso.trans_hom`, and
395	  -- `pullbackSymmetry`/`pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`/`pullback.congrHom` to a
396	  -- 6-factor `.app ⊤` chain), but the residual `r_1.appTop(isLocElem) = 1` fact
397	  -- (Step 1) does NOT discharge from the abstract `r_1, h_r_1` hypotheses alone.
398	  -- The reason: `(Proj.awayι _).appTop : Γ(Proj 𝒜, ⊤) → Γ(Spec(Away 𝒜 X_1), ⊤)`
399	  -- has image = `kbar ⊂ Away 𝒜 X_1` (the degree-0 part), which does NOT contain
400	  -- `isLocElem = X_0/X_1`. So `h_r_1` (which gives `r_1.appTop ∘ (Proj.awayι).appTop
401	  -- = onePt.left.appTop`) cannot directly compute `r_1.appTop(isLocElem)`.
402	  --
403	  -- The computation requires the EXPLICIT cancel_mono uniqueness `r_1 =
404	  -- IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayι _) onePt.left h_range` (auto from `h_r_1` +
405	  -- `Mono (Proj.awayι _)`), then `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` of that lift on the
406	  -- preimage of `D₊(X_1) ⊆ Proj 𝒜` to evaluate `r_1.appTop(isLocElem)` as
407	  -- `(Proj.awayι _).appIso ⊤ .inv (...) ≫ onePt.left.app (...)` applied to isLocElem.
408	  -- The image of `isLocElem ∈ Γ(Spec(Away 𝒜 X_1), ⊤)` under the `appIso.inv`
409	  -- direction is the `[X_0/X_1] ∈ Γ(Proj 𝒜, D₊(X_1))` section; then `onePt.left.app`
410	  -- applied to it sends `[X_0/X_1] ↦ v(X_0)/v(X_1)` where `v = (1, 1) ∈ k̄²`, giving 1.

187	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
188	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=

191	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
192	          (fun _ : Unit =>
193	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
194	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)
195	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
196	              (match i with
197	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
198	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
199	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
200	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
201	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
202	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
203	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫

208	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
209	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
210	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
211	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
212	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
213	
214	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
215	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
216	
217	**Status (iter-180):** body retired axiom-clean via the empirically-verified
218	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` recipe of
219	`analogies/pullbackspeciso-bypass.md` (Decision 4). The option collapses the
220	`Algebra.compHom`-driven heartbeat sink on the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite
221	that blocked iter-175 through iter-179. -/
222	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
223	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
224	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
225	  -- Stage 1 (iter-179): unfold the chart map, apply `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` after a
226	  -- type-realigning `change`, and collapse the right-hand `Spec.map` chain into
155:private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :

130	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
131	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
132	        GmRing kbar))
133	
134	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
135	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
136	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
137	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
138	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
139	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
140	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
141	
142	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
143	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
144	
145	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
146	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
147	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
148	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
149	
150	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
151	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
152	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
153	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
154	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
155	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
156	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
157	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
158	        (TensorProduct kbar
159	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
160	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
161	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
162	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
163	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
164	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
165	    pullback.congrHom
166	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
167	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
168	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
169	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
170	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
171	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
172	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
173	
174	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
175	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
176	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
177	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
178	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
179	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
180	
181	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
182	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
183	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
184	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
185	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
186	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
187	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
188	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
189	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫

  - `(· + ·)` is shorthand for `fun x y => x + y`
  - `(f · a b)` is shorthand for `fun x => f x a b`

# AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean — iter-191 Lane E

## Summary

- **HARD BAR met**: Part 1 (refactor `iotaGm_chart1_section` +
  `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` to specialise on
  `iotaGm_r_1` / `iotaGm_r_1_fac`) **LANDED axiom-clean**.
- **Part 2** (body close) **DEFERRED** at the 80 LOC HARD BUDGET
  wall per PROGRESS.md sub-step scope discipline + progress-critic
  STUCK verdict.
- **Sorry count**: 2 → 2 (unchanged). Lines shifted to 265, 775.
- **Build**: GREEN. File compiles cleanly.
- **Net file delta**: −20 LOC raw (abstract `r_1, h_r_1` parameters
  removed from `iotaGm_chart1_section` and the composition lemma);
  +25 LOC documentation explaining the iter-191 refactor + iter-192
  deferral plan.

## Part 1 — `iotaGm_chart1_section` + `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` refactor (line 178+)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach** (per PROGRESS.md slot 3 Part 1 + iter-190 review §7):
  Drop abstract `(r_1, h_r_1)` parameters from both
  `iotaGm_chart1_section` and the composition lemma; substitute
  `iotaGm_r_1 kbar` / `iotaGm_r_1_fac kbar` directly. Update the
  downstream consumer `iotaGm_isOpenImmersion` to call the new
  parameter-free signatures.
- **Result**: **RESOLVED** — refactor lands, file compiles, sorry
  count preserved.
- **Key structural change**:
  - `iotaGm_chart1_section` signature: `(kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X (1 : Fin 2)`. The
    `(Gm kbar).hom ≫ iotaGm_r_1 kbar` is now embedded directly in the
    `pullback.lift`, and the compatibility proof uses
    `iotaGm_r_1_fac kbar` directly.
  - `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` signature:
    `[IsAlgClosed kbar] : IsOpenImmersion (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar
    ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2))`. No more
    `(r_1, h_r_1)` parameters.
  - `iotaGm_isOpenImmersion` consumer: dropped the `have h_r_1 :=
    iotaGm_r_1_fac kbar` step; calls
    `iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion (kbar := kbar)`
    parameter-free.
- **Why it matters**: After the refactor, the section body contains
  `iotaGm_r_1 kbar` directly, which by definition is
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift (Proj.awayι ...) onePt.left _`. So
  `unfold iotaGm_r_1` exposes the `IsOpenImmersion.lift` shape ready
  for `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` — no `cancel_mono` uniqueness step
  required to certify `r_1` as a `lift`. This is the key new hook
  that Part 2 (iter-192+) will leverage.

## Part 2 — body close (line 432; DEFERRED)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach** (per PROGRESS.md slot 3 Part 2): after Part 1's
  specialisation, apply `unfold iotaGm_r_1` to expose
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift`, then `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` to evaluate
  the appTop component on `isLocalizationElem`, then
  `Proj.basicOpenIsoAway` + `IsLocalization.map` on chart-1 coord
  to identify `isLocElem ↦ 1 ∈ kbar`.
- **Result**: **PARTIAL — deferred at HARD BUDGET wall** per
  PROGRESS.md sub-step scope discipline.
- **Diagnostic**: Tested via `lean_multi_attempt` at the sorry
  position:
  - `simp` (default): reduces to the same 6-factor `.app ⊤` chain
    as the iter-188 simp telescope — no progress past the iter-190
    structural floor.
  - `simp [pullback.lift_*, pullbackSpecIso_inv_*, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_*, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, Scheme.Hom.appTop]`:
    **all simp arguments flagged unused** — confirms `simp` alone
    cannot drive the `pullbackSpecIso_inv` / `pullbackSymmetry`
    rewrites with the goal in its present form (the iso factors
    are on the LHS, not exposed as `.fst`/`.snd` projections).
  - `unfold iotaGm_r_1; rw [IsOpenImmersion.lift_app]`: `unfold`
    succeeds (confirms the Part 1 hook), but the `rw` for
    `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` does not find a syntactic match
    pattern — the `iotaGm_r_1` use sits behind a chain of pullback
    iso `.hom .app ⊤`s and is not at the head of the goal.
- **Dead end**: pure-simp closure on the 6-factor `.app ⊤` chain.
  The genuine substantive gap is the `r_1.appTop(isLocElem) = 1`
  computation, which requires the `Proj.appIso` evaluation
  identifying `isLocElem ∈ Γ(Spec(Away 𝒜(X_1)), ⊤)` with the
  section `[X_0/X_1] ∈ Γ(Proj 𝒜, D₊(X_1))`, then evaluating
  `onePt.left.app` on it. This is the same residual that was
  STUCK iter-188 / iter-189 / iter-190 — a depth of `Proj.appIso`
  machinery that exceeds the iter-191 sub-step 80 LOC budget.
- **Next step (iter-192+)** per PROGRESS.md Failure Mode for Lane E:
  plan-phase dispatch `blueprint-writer
  avr-chart1-composition-expand` to expand the chapter's sketch on
  the `Proj.appIso` evaluation step (`isLocElem ↦ [X_0/X_1] ↦ 1`)
  BEFORE re-attempting the body close. The Part 1 refactor's new
  hook (`iotaGm_r_1` directly an `IsOpenImmersion.lift`) is
  preserved for the iter-192 attempt; the residual now hangs on
  the `Proj.appIso` machinery, no longer on the lift-identification
  step.

## Lemmas / structural observations

- **`IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`** (Mathlib): pattern is
  `Scheme.Hom.app (IsOpenImmersion.lift f g H) V = (Scheme.Hom.appIso
  f V).inv ≫ ...`. Requires the LHS to syntactically expose the
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift f g H` at the head of `.app`. After
  iter-191 Part 1 refactor, this pattern is now reachable from
  `iotaGm_r_1` via `unfold` — but the goal at the sorry has
  `iotaGm_r_1` sitting behind a chain of pullback iso `.hom .app ⊤`s,
  so reaching it requires a `pullback.lift_snd`-style descent first.
- **`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen`**: used in
  `iotaGm_r_1_range_subset` body (iter-184 closure). The iter-192+
  closure of Part 2 can lean on the analogous `Proj.appIso`
  evaluation lemma — search query for iter-192 prover: "evaluate
  `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` of `Proj.awayι` composed with a
  `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` source on the basic open `D₊(X_1)`"
  (the iter-189 mathlib-analogist directive sketched in the
  iter-188 comment block above line 432).

## Files touched

- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` — refactor +
  documentation update (no other Lean files modified).

## Blueprint markers

- `chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` — CLEARED (no changes
  needed; the Part 1 refactor preserves all declaration names and
  the iter-192 Part 2 plan is consistent with the chapter's
  existing sketch).

## Recommendation

- Part 1 axiom-clean closure is the iter-191 HARD BAR. **MET.**
- Per progress-critic STUCK + PROGRESS.md Failure Mode, the
  iter-192 plan-phase should dispatch
  `blueprint-writer avr-chart1-composition-expand` to expand the
  chapter's sketch on the `Proj.appIso` evaluation step BEFORE the
  iter-192 prover attempts Part 2.
